description:
The GridLayout component aligns the UIX elements from left to right, top to bottom. This component also comes with controllable parameters that a user can use to adjust the way the grid layout is represented on the Canvas or element slot.

}}

fields.PaddingTop: Makes padding on the top of this grid, separating it from the top.
fields.PaddingRight: Makes padding on the right of this grid, separating it from the right.
fields.PaddingBottom: Makes padding on the bottom of this grid, separating it from the bottom.
fields.PaddingLeft: Makes padding on the left of this grid, separating it from the left.
fields.CellSize: The size of each content cell.
fields.Spacing: Makes padding between the content cells of this grid, separating it evenly.
fields.HorizontalAlign: how this grid should be aligned horizontally.
fields.VerticalAlign: how this grid should be aligned vertically.
fields.ExpandWidthToFit: Extends this grid to fit the entire UIX element that it is on.
fields.PreserveAspectOnExpand: Preserves the aspect ratio when expanded to fit.
fields.AlignLastRowIndividually: Alighns the last row for this grid seprately.

usage: This is used to make a nice grid layout of a user's content. The user has a lot of control when it comes to making this kind of layout, especially when it comes to the amount of content cells, expanding and scaling those cells, and alignments.
